_comment: Cross-language test vector for the Langy authenticated frame contract (LANGY_WORKER_REDESIGN_PLAN.md §0a). The Go worker SIGNS frames; the TS Hono relay VERIFIES them. Both MUST reproduce these MACs byte-for-byte. Regenerate with langy-frame-auth.vectors.gen.mjs — the generator is an INDEPENDENT reference (raw node:crypto), not either implementation under test, so a passing suite proves Go ≡ TS ≡ oracle.
construction: mac = hex( HMAC-SHA256( key = hexDecode(runToken), signingInput ) ) where signingInput = concat, over the fields [projectId, userId, conversationId, turnId, frameNonce, payload] in that fixed order, of uint32BE(utf8ByteLength(field)) followed by utf8(field). Length-prefixing makes the field concatenation injective: no byte can be shifted across a field boundary to forge a colliding tuple.

fieldShift.note: Proves length-prefixing works: shifting one byte across the projectId/userId boundary yields a DIFFERENT mac. a.mac != b.mac.
